rulings.Case Title: IN RE: STATE FARM MUTUAL AUTOMBILE INSURANCE COMPANY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Compel Arbitration; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Guy O. Kornblum In Support Of Respondents' Petition
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, January 3, 2019, Line 1, RESPONDENTs DEBORAH DENOIA, CHARLES HEMMINGER Petition To Compel Arbitration; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Guy O. Kornblum In Support Of Respondents' Petition Respondents Deborah DeNoia and Charles Hemminger's petition to compel arbitration is granted and the hearing is continued to January 16, 2019 for the appointment of an arbitrator in the event that the parties cannot agree before then regarding the person to be selected as their arbitrator. The parties are welcome, but not required, to submit names of proposed arbitrators to the court by email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties no later than January 14, 2019. The arbitrator and the parties should determine a schedule for the arbitration, which should not be arbitrarily imposed by the court. Petitioner State Farm Mutual Automobile Insurance Company has not provided any persuasive reason why the arbitration should not move forward expeditiously. If State Farm believes that it needs further time for discovery, State Farm should so inform the arbitrator. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: PONIST LAW GROUP P.C. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petition For Appointment Of Neutral Arbitration [Ccp Section 1281.6}; Mem Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, January 3, 2019, Line 3, PETITIONER PONIST LAW GROUP P.C., SEAN PONIST's Hearing On Petition For Appointment Of Neutral Arbitration [Ccp Section 1281.6}; Mem Of Points And Authorities Petitioners Ponist Law Group, P.C. and Sean E. Ponist's petition to appoint an arbitrator is granted. Marilyn O'Toole is appointed as the parties' arbitrator. The first backup arbitrator is Richard Idell. The second backup arbitrator is Bruce Methven. Respondents were properly served with the petition per CCP section 1290.4 (a) because the parties' agreement provides that arbitration shall be conducted under the rules of the AAA and rule 43 of the AAA Commercial Arbitration Rules provides that service of process may be effected by personal or mail delivery to the party or its representatives. Therefore, service on respondents' representatives in the arbitration proceedings by personal delivery and overnight mail or email constitutes proper service. All of the arguments in respondents' most recent memorandum lack merit and were either rejected or should have been raised at the November 29, 2018 hearing. Significantly, respondents fail to identify any AAA rules that might possibly be applicable that are in any way different than rule 43 of the AAA Commercial Arbitration Rules. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for petitioners is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: LAURA ALBERT ET AL VS. INTERNATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Leave To Conduct Psychiatric Ime And Testing Of Trevor Knoop, For Appointment Of A Special Master, And For Order Setting Plaintiff Knoops Deposition
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, January 3, 2019, Line 1, DEFENDANTS INTERNATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL, JANE CAMBLIN'S Motion For Leave To Conduct Psychiatric Ime And Testing Of Trevor Knoop, For Appointment Of A Special Master, And For Order Setting Plaintiff Knoops Deposition. Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: To be heard on the same day at 9:30 am in the Law and Motion Dept. 302 before Judge Kahn. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@sbslawsf.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: SAN FRANCISCO INDEPENDENT TAXI ASSOCIATION VS. APPLIED UNDERWRITERS, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, January 3, 2019, Line 6, CROSS DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO INDEPENDENT TAXI ASSOCIATION's DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT Cross-defendant San Francisco Independent Taxi Association's demurrer to all three causes of action in the cross-complaint is sustained without leave to amend as to the first cause of action for breach of contract by Applied Underwriters Captive Risk Assurance Company, Inc. and overruled as to the second and third causes of action for breach of contract and quasi-contract by California Insurance Company. The Reinsurance Participation Agreement is illegal and unenforceable as a matter of law because it is a collateral agreement that modifies the obligations of the insured and was not filed with the California Department of Insurance as required by Insurance Code section 11658 and 10 CCR 2268(b)). While Nielsen Contracting v. Applied Underwriters, Inc. (2018) 22 Cal.App.5th 1096 did not reach the issue of the RPA's validity beyond the delegation clause and the arbitration provision, the logic of that decision extends to the entire RPA. Although the Insurance Commissioner's determinations in Matter of Shasta Linen Supply, Inc. are not binding, that case provides persuasive reasoning that the RPA is unenforceable as a matter of law. The two Shasta Linen decisions by Judge Shubb held that the rates in the RPA are not invalid per Insurance Code sections 11735 and 11737 because the Insurance Commissioner had not expressly disapproved the rates. Judge Shubb's decisions did not express any view regarding the validity of the RPA per section 11658. Because the RPA deviates from the CIC policies in many respects not just limited to rates, the RPA is an impermissible collateral agreement that is unenforceable in its entirety. CIC sufficiently alleged both a breach of contract claim under the policies and an alternative quasi-contract claim in the event that the contracts that form the bases of the breach of contract claim are determined to be unenforceable. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for SFITA is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: JASON TRAVIS VS. HARRIETT M WILSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Defendant Harriet M. Wilson To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set Two; Request For Order Awarding Sanctions Against Plaintiff And His Attorney In The Sum Of $1,320.00; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, January 3, 2019, Line 6, DEFENDANTS HARRIETT WILSON, THE HARRIETT M. WILSON TRUST'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set Two; Request For Order Awarding Sanctions Against Plaintiff And His Attorney In The Sum Of $1,320.00. (Specially set for 2:00 p.m. by Judge Pro Tem Bruce Highman) Pro Tem Judge Bruce Highman, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Denied as to interrogatories 97, 103, 104, 112, 117, 122, 132, 133, 138, 141, 142 and 150. The information requested by these interrogatories was sufficiently requested by other interrogatories. The motion is otherwise granted. The interrogatories as to which the motion is granted are not sufficiently the same as the prior discovery requests plaintiff cites. Among other things, the court notes that: (1) the plaintiff answers deposition questions off the top of his head but has to do appropriate investigation when answering an interrogatory; and (2) one can't necessarily tell from responses to document requests which documents are being produced in response to which document request, so interrogatories asking for documents to be identified are appropriate. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to bruce.highman@highmanlaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: NORMA SANCHEZ-ZAPATA VS. ROKA AKOR SAN FRANCISCO LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendants Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, January 3, 2019, Line 7, DEFENDANT ROKA AKOR SAN FRANCISCO LLC MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendants' Motion For Summary Judgment Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication Defendant Roka Akor San Francisco LLC's motion for summary judgment and alternative motion for summary adjudication as to plaintiff Norma Sanchez-Zapata's first amended complaint are denied in their entirety. As to each cause of action, there is triable dispute whether Roka Akor's alleged legitimate business reasons for the adverse employment actions were pretextual. The temporal proximity of the adverse employment actions and the arguable contradictions in Roka Akor's positions create triable issues. The fourth issue sought to be summarily adjudicated (whether Ms. Sanchez-Zapata can recover economic damages) does not completely dispose of an entire cause of action and thus cannot be summarily adjudicated per CCP section 437c(f)(1). The only damages issue that can be summarily adjudicated is a request for exemplary damages. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Ms. Sanchez-Zapata is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: DARA AUYEUNG VS. THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, January 3, 2019, Line 11, DEFENDANT THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT Judge Kahn discloses that the General Counsel of the University of California is a close friend of his, but neither that friendship nor any other matter causes him to be anything other than fair and impartial in deciding this case. Defendant The Regents of the University of California's demurrer to the first four causes of action in the first amended complaint filed by plaintiff Dara Auyeung is off calendar as moot as to the first cause of action for violation of Labor Code section 970, sustained with twenty days leave to amend as to the second cause of action for fraud, and overruled with as to the third and fourth causes of action for breach of contract and breach of the implied covenant. Apparently Ms. Auyeung has or will dismiss the first cause of action. The Regents have absolute immunity as to the fraud claim per Government Code section 818.8. Ms. Auyeung is given leave to name individual defendants on her fraud claim per Government Code section 822.2. A public entity can be liable on contractual employment claims. (Retired Employees Assoc. of Orange County v.County of Orange (2011) 52 Cal.4th 1171, 1182-83.) The Regents' effort in their reply memorandum to distinguish and limit Retired Employees is not supported by any language in Retired Employees or in any other case cited in the reply memorandum. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the Regents is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk
